bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting my maximum-utility bid. The opponent opened with all valueF, which is very low for me and provides no evidence of meaningful concession yet, so I will counter firmly on my highest-preferred values.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a small concession to avoid simply repeating my prior all-best offer. The opponent has held firm at all valueF, which is very low utility for me and does not yet show meaningful concession, so I will only soften on issueC from valueA to valueB while keeping my highest-weight issueA at valueA.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early at t=0.04 and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeating the same all-valueF offer that is very poor for me. To avoid simply repeating my last bid while still holding firm on the most important issueA and other high-value terms, I make a small concession on issueC only.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.836, since it is still very early at t=0.06 and the opponent has not conceded at all from their all-valueF demand. I continue making only a small concession on the lower-weight issueC while holding firm on my highest-priority issueA and other important valueA positions; there are no opponent messages and no valid counter-signal beyond their repeated extreme offer.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.86 because it is still very early in the negotiation and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeatedly offering all valueF, which is very poor for me. I will avoid repeating my immediately previous bid while keeping my highest-weight issues, especially issueA, at my preferred value and making only a small concession on issueC.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 because it is still very early at t=0.10 and there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent has repeatedly offered the same all-valueF bid, which is very poor for me and shows no concession, so I will hold firm on my most important issues while varying issueC slightly to avoid repeating my last bid.

I am targeting a high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.12 and the opponent has shown no concession at all, repeatedly offering the same all-valueF bid. I will avoid repeating my immediately previous offer while keeping firm on my highest-value issues, especially issueA, issueB, and issueD, and only making a small concession on issueC.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 because it is still early at t=0.14 and the opponent has shown no concession, repeatedly offering all valueF. Since their behavior gives no reliable signal of tradeoffs, I will hold firm on the highest-weight issueA and keep strong values on issueB and issueD, while making only a modest concession on issueC and avoiding my immediately previous exact bid.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.86 because it is still early at t=0.16 and the opponent has shown no concession, repeatedly offering the same all-valueF bid. Since their behavior gives no useful tradeoff signal beyond extreme firmness, I will continue holding my most important issueA and other high-value terms while varying my prior offer to avoid simply repeating the last bid.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still early at t=0.18 and the opponent has shown no concession, repeatedly offering the all-valueF bid that is very poor for me. To avoid simply repeating my last offer while still conceding only modestly, I keep my most important issueA and other high-value positions firm and vary issueC to a still-valuable alternative.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.856 by holding firm on the most important issueA and preserving strong values on issueB and issueD, while making only a small concession on issueC. At t=0.20 it is still early, and the opponent has shown no concession at all, repeatedly offering all valueF, so there is no evidence yet that a larger concession would be reciprocated.

I am still targeting a high-utility bid because we are early in the negotiation at t=0.22 and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeatedly offering all valueF, which is very low for me. To avoid repeating my last exact bid while making only a modest concession, I vary issueC while holding firm on the most important issueA and other high-value positions.

At t=0.24 there is still substantial time left, and the opponent has shown no concession at all, repeatedly offering all valueF, which is very low utility for me. I am targeting a high-utility bid while avoiding an exact repeat of my last offer, making only a modest concession on issueC while holding firm on my most important issueA and other strong values.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.837, keeping my strongest outcomes on issueB, issueC, and issueD while making a modest concession on issueA to avoid repeating my last offer. Since it is still early at t=0.26 and the opponent has held completely firm on all-F offers with no concession, I should not move close to their position yet, but I can test a small tradeoff using a valid value.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around the early/mid-game range, since t=0.28 is still not late enough to justify major concessions. The opponent has offered the same all-valueF bid every time and has shown no concession pattern, so I will avoid moving close to that while still varying my offer from my immediately previous bid.

I am targeting a strong but somewhat conciliatory bid around 0.77 utility by holding my top values on issueA, issueB, and issueC while conceding issueD to the opponent’s repeatedly demanded valueF. The opponent has made no concessions at all and keeps offering all valueF, so at t=0.30 I will not move close to their full demand, but I will test a meaningful concession on one lower-priority issue.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.76, conceding one issue to valueF while holding firm on high-value positions, especially issueA and issueD. At t=0.32 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s unchanged all-valueF demand; their history shows no concession, so I am testing a different single-issue concession rather than repeating my last bid.

I am targeting a strong utility around 0.74: still well above reservation, but with a meaningful concession on issueC to test whether the opponent is anchored on valueF. Time is only at 0.34, so I should not make deep concessions yet, especially since the opponent has repeated the same all-F offer without any observable movement. Their behavior suggests they are holding firm rather than conceding, so I will avoid repeating my last bid while still protecting the highest-value issues for me.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.63 by conceding on issueB and issueD, which are less costly concessions than giving up issueA or issueC. At t=0.36 there is some pressure to move beyond single-issue concessions, but it is not late enough to accept the opponent’s all-valueF demand, especially since they have shown no concession at all and sent no messages.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.57: a meaningful concession from my earlier near-maximal bids, but still well above the opponent’s repeated all-valueF offer to me. Since it is still only t=0.38 and the opponent has shown no concession at all, I will not give up my most important issueA, but I will concede on lower-cost issues they appear to demand as valueF.

At t=0.40 I am targeting a moderate concession while preserving my highest-weight issueA at its best value, since the opponent has held firm on all valueF offers and has not shown any reciprocal concession. Because it is mid-negotiation rather than late, I will not move all the way to their repeated bid, but I will concede on lower/medium-weight issues to test whether a bid closer to their apparent preference can gain traction.

I am targeting a moderate concession bid around 0.47 utility, preserving my most important issueA at valueA while conceding the lower-weight issues toward the opponent's repeated valueF demand. Since time is in the mid-game and the opponent has held completely firm on all valueF offers with no messages, I am making a stronger move toward their apparent preference without accepting their very low all-F bid.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.41 by holding less than ideal but still positive value, since we are nearing mid-negotiation and the opponent has shown no concession at all, repeatedly offering all valueF. Their behavior suggests they strongly prefer valueF across issues, so I keep valueF on issueB, issueC, and issueD, while making a further concession on issueA rather than repeating my last exact bid.

I am targeting a utility around 0.47 by conceding issueB, issueC, and issueD to valueF while holding firm on issueA, my highest-weight issue. The opponent has repeatedly offered all valueF with no concession, and there are no messages to change that read; at t=0.46 it is still too early to drop all the way to their low-utility offer.

I targeted a utility around 0.405 by conceding issueB, issueC, and issueD to the opponent’s repeatedly signaled valueF while still preserving some value on my highest-weight issueA. At t=0.48 there is moderate time pressure, but not enough to accept their all-valueF bid; the opponent has shown no concession at all and sent no messages, so I am making a measured concession without dropping to my reservation-level outcome.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.47 by holding firm on issueA, my highest-weight issue, while conceding the other issues to the opponent’s repeatedly signaled valueF position. At t=0.50 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept their all-valueF bid, especially since they have shown no concession at all and no messages provide additional credible information. To avoid repeating my immediately previous bid, I switch issueA back to valueA while keeping issueB/issueC/issueD at valueF.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.405 by holding some value on my highest-weight issueA while conceding the other issues to the opponent's repeatedly signaled preference of valueF. At t=0.52 there is meaningful time pressure, but the opponent has shown no concession at all and has only offered all valueF, so I should not collapse fully to their bid yet. This bid also avoids repeating my immediately previous offer while moving toward what their history suggests they may accept.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.44 by holding firm on my highest-weight issueA while conceding heavily on the other issues. At t=0.54 there is time pressure to move closer to the opponent, but the opponent has mostly held at valueF and only shifted issueD to valueE, so I am matching that signal on the lower-cost issueD while preserving value on issueA.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.375 by preserving some value on the most important issueA while conceding heavily on issueB and issueC and also matching the opponent’s brief signal on issueD=valueE. At t=0.56 there is meaningful time pressure, and the opponent has mostly held firm on valueF with only one move to issueD=valueE, so I am making a larger concession without dropping all the way to their repeated F/F/F/F demand.

I am targeting a moderate remaining utility around 0.31 by conceding heavily on issues B, C, and D while still preserving some value on the highest-weight issueA. The opponent has been essentially fixed on all valueF, with no meaningful concession, so at t=0.58 I should move closer to their apparent demand but not yet drop all the way to their exact low-utility offer.

I am targeting a low but still positive utility around 0.28 because we are past the midpoint and the opponent has remained very rigid, repeatedly offering mostly valueF and recently signaling issueD: valueE. Since their offers show almost no concession toward my preferences, I will concede on low/medium-priority issues they appear to demand while preserving some value on my most important issueA.

I am targeting a modest utility around 0.31 by conceding heavily on issueB and issueC while preserving some value on the most important issueA. At t=0.62 there is increasing time pressure, but the opponent has mostly held firm at valueF and only varied issueD, so I will match their preferred valueF on issueD rather than the worse valueE and avoid repeating my immediately previous bid.

At t=0.64 the opponent has been almost completely firm on issueA/issueB/issueC = valueF, with only small movement between valueF and valueE on issueD, so I am prioritizing agreement over holding out. I target a low but still positive utility bid that matches the opponent’s repeatedly signaled acceptable package, choosing issueD: valueF because it is better for me than their latest valueE while still consistent with many of their past offers.
